headline: June payrolls only 57,000; unemployment 4.2%
detail: A sharp jobs miss signals the economy is cooling faster than expected. For your chip and tech-heavy book, this cuts both ways: Fed rate-cut bets rise (good for growth multiples) but real demand fears for semiconductors and enterprise software can outweigh that in the short term. On margin, a risk-off open would hurt SOXL and SPAL hardest.
rec: No new buys triggered by this. Watch SOXL near its $200 stop closely at the open - if semis gap down on growth fears, that stop could be tested fast. No scanner setup provides enough conviction to add risk into this uncertainty.

headline: Tesla crushes Q2 delivery estimates
detail: Tesla delivering 480,126 vehicles and beating expectations lifts sentiment across high-growth, momentum-driven names - the same category your QQQ, SMH, and SOXL sit in. It signals consumer demand for big-ticket tech products isn't completely dead despite the macro softness. Positive read for overall tech sentiment today.
rec: You already hold QQQ and SMH which benefit from a positive sentiment lift. No specific new buy is warranted here on this headline alone - let your existing holds capture any upside.

headline: Nvidia betting on trillion-dollar robotics boom
detail: Nvidia's robotics push is directly relevant to your chip exposure via SMH and SOXL. If Nvidia continues to expand its TAM narrative, it supports the bull case for semiconductor ETFs. However this is a thematic story, not an immediate catalyst.
rec: No action needed today. If SMH stabilizes above $540 stop and the jobs softness doesn't spark a selloff, your existing SMH position is the cleanest way to stay exposed. TSM shows a scanner setup (entry 445.86, stop 442.77) but daily bias is mixed - not compelling enough to add new margin risk today.

headline: Jeff Bezos family office backs five AI startups in June
detail: Continued big-money flow into AI startups reinforces the secular AI spending theme that underpins your CRWD, SMH, and QQQ exposure. It doesn't move markets today but confirms the narrative driving your core holdings remains intact.
rec: No trade warranted. This is background confirmation of the theme you're already invested in - no new position or addition is justified from this headline alone.
